channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Not exactly a DeepSeek moment for AI accelerators - but this Chinese optical chip may well be 100x faster than Nvidia's A100 on some tasks
item.description: Chinese photonic AI chips demonstrate extreme speed and energy efficiency in narrowly defined generative workloads, exceeding conventional GPU performance in lab tests.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: How to watch Australia vs England: live stream 5th Ashes Test for *FREE* from anywhere, Day 1
item.description: All the ways to watch 5th Ashes Test live streams for FREE from anywhere, as Australia and England face off in the series finale at Sydney Cricket Ground.
item.dc:creator: Aatif Sulleyman, TechRadar

item.title: A California lawmaker wants to ban AI from children’s toys
item.description: A California lawmaker has introduced a first-in-the-nation bill meant to ban companies from embedding AI chatbot technology into toys designed for children.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: Opinion: EPA helping AI industry grow but ignoring technology’s power to protect environment
item.description: Agency must use artificial intelligence to defend communities with same zeal it applies to cutting protections that safeguard them.
item.dc:creator: Avi Garbow, The Mercury News

item.title: How to share your estimated arrival time on iPhone and Android
item.description: ETA sharing on Apple Maps and Google Maps keeps contacts updated automatically while you drive safely. Learn step-by-step instructions for iPhone and Android.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China requires chipmakers to use at least 50% domestic equipment
item.description: According to Reuters, China is now requiring its chipmakers to use at least 50% domestically made equipment when expanding their production capacity. The rule is not publicly documented, but chipmakers must show that at least half of their equipment is Chinese to get government approval.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Here’s how the US economy fared under Trump in 2025
item.description: President Donald Trump rode a wave of voter frustration about the economy to a second term in the White House, promising lower prices and a "golden age" for the nation.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Wispr CEO: What a post-keyboard office might look like
item.description: Voice-to-text tools have been around for decades, yet there’s little sign that office workers are relying any less on their keyboards.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: From integration pain to partnership gain: How collaboration strengthens cybersecurity
item.description: There are several data-related barriers that can limit collaboration gains. Unsurprisingly, and with good reason, fear of inadvertently compromising systems stands at the top of the list, especially when external partners are involved.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI agents 2025 recap: What happened and what to expect next year
item.description: In artificial intelligence, 2025 marked a decisive shift. Systems once confined to research labs and prototypes began to appear as everyday tools. At the center of this transition was the rise of AI agents—AI systems that can use other software tools and act on their own.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI tightens AI rules for teens but concerns remain
item.description: OpenAI announces new teen safety rules for ChatGPT users under 18, blocking romantic roleplay and requiring extra caution on body image topics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delivering resilience and continuity for AI
item.description: Infrastructure might be the reason so many organizations report failures when scaling AI from POC to production.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to build RAG at scale
item.description: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has quickly become the enterprise default for grounding generative AI in internal knowledge. It promises less hallucination, more accuracy, and a way to unlock value from decades of documents, policies, tickets, and institutional memory.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Understanding AI-native cloud: from microservices to model-serving
item.description: Cloud computing has fundamentally transformed the way enterprises operate. Initially built for more basic, everyday computing tasks, its capabilities have expanded exponentially with the advent of new technologies (such as machine learning and analytics).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SoftBank expands AI infrastructure ambitions with $4B DigitalBridge acquisition
item.description: SoftBank Group has announced plans to acquire DigitalBridge Group, an asset manager specializing in digital infrastructure investments such as data centers, cell towers, and fiber networks, in a deal valued at $4 billion.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: React2Shell: Anatomy of a max-severity flaw that sent shockwaves through the web
item.description: The React 19 library for building application interfaces was hit with a remote code vulnerability, React2Shell, about a month ago. However, as researchers delve deeper into the bug, the larger picture gradually unravels.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI looks for a new head of AI preparedness
item.description: OpenAI has announced that it is looking for a new Head of Preparedness, who will be responsible for anticipating and mitigating the risks and damages that its AI models can cause. As compensation, OpenAI is offering around $550,000 in annual salary, plus stock.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 4 New Year’s resolutions for devops success
item.description: It has been a dramatic and challenging year for developers and engineers working in devops organizations. More companies are using AI and automation for both development and IT operations, including for writing requirements, maintaining documentation, and vibe coding.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI’s trust tax for developers
item.description: Andrej Karpathy is one of the few people in this industry who has earned the right to be listened to without a filter. As a founding member of OpenAI and the former director of AI at Tesla, he sits at the summit of AI and its possibilities.
item.dc:creator: Info World
